vulnerability.reference: Reliance on miscalibrated models for high-stakes decisions produces inequitable resource allocations.
vulnerability.generated: Reliance on simulation-free COT models without built-in calibration diagnostics for Bayesian inverse problem solving.

impact.reference: Disadvantaged groups suffer from misallocated interventions due to flawed model confidence.
impact.generated: Overconfident posterior samples mislead scientific inference and engineering decisions, degrading reliability in applications like drug dosage prediction.

dose_response.reference: Miscalibrated conditional transport plans cause overconfident posterior samples in COT-based inverse models, leading to misallocated resources that disproportionately harm disadvantaged groups.
dose_response.generated: Miscalibrated conditional transport plans, through their use by scientists and engineers in COT-based inverse problem solving, trigger reliance on simulation-free models lacking calibration diagnostics, leading to overconfident posterior samples misleading scientific inference and engineering decisions, degrading drug dosage prediction reliability.
